content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach makes multiple specific, timely reads that change the direction of the conversation. Turn 4 catches the linguistic signature of victimhood ('to me'). Turn 8 names a specific behavioral read — the trail-off — and inquires into it. Turn 10 catches the intellectualization-as-exit and gently redirects to body. Turn 12 catches the deflecting laugh-and-dismiss ('that's insane') and refuses it, anchoring back to the specific somatic phrase the client offered. Turn 14 names the explaining-away pattern directly ('Yeah, you are'). Reads consistently precede questions, and they track linguistic caveats, deflection signatures, and the intellectual exit — all AoA-specific reads. The coach distinguishes the said from the shown throughout.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the quality of the question itself — wondering vs. accusation — rather than taking the content at face value."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic signature 'to me' and names it before posing the suspect question. Read precedes inquiry."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Pivots to feeling-quality question but doesn't explicitly name a read here; reasonable de-escalation after pushback."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Specific behavioral read of the trail-off — names what she did ('you trailed off') and inquires into the unsaid word."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Implicitly catches the intellectualizing exit and redirects to body ('what was actually happening in your body'), staying with the specific word 'flat'."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the self-dismissal ('that's not insane') and anchors back to the specific somatic image she gave — distinguishing what she said from what she's doing with it."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the explaining-away pattern directly ('Yeah, you are') — meta-read of her deflection mechanism in real time."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["looking-for-whats-wrong"],
  "notes": "Coach does not explicitly name the anxious-avoidant pattern or the 'looking for what's wrong' frame, but the specific somatic/linguistic reads are consistent and well-timed. The work is happening at the level of micro-reads rather than pattern-level reads, which is appropriate given where the client is."
}
